In the analysis of the data, Model 1 shows the following results: for Gender, the coefficient (B) is 0.357 with a standard error
(SE) of 0.103, yielding an odds ratio (OR) of 1.43; Ethnicity has B of −0.205, SE of 0.103, and OR of 0.82; Body weight
reflects B of 0.629, SE of 0.103, and OR of 1.88; Thiamin is not applicable; Energy is not applicable; Carbohydrate is not
applicable; Blood Glucose is not applicable; Blood Cholesterol is not applicable; Blood Triglycerides is not applicable; and
Blood Albumin is not applicable. In Model 2, results indicate that for Gender, B is 0.291 with an SE of 0.107 and an OR of

Thiamin yields B of −0.103, SE of 0.112, and OR of 0.90; Energy shows B of 0.142, SE of 0.149, and OR of 1.15;
Carbohydrate has B of −0.325, SE of 0.146, and OR of 0.72; Blood Glucose is not applicable; Blood Cholesterol is not
applicable; Blood Triglycerides is not applicable; and Blood Albumin is not applicable. In Model 3, Gender presents B of 0.429,
